title: The Potion to Izzy's Attic
summary: Seeker and Uncle Crisco drink a magical potion to travel to Izzy's house. After a cozy night spent on unique beds like a cheese mouse pillow and a bush bed, the group explores an attic filled with Legos and art supplies. They spend the morning building a variety of robots and drawing pictures of magical creatures and skyships before the travelers return home using a vanishing potion.

highlightQuote.text: Yes, she can, because she has a mouth.

transcript.text: Alright. Let's - can you please put that on the light so that we can make some stars?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: I was just wearing it to the light.
There we go. Oh, there we go. We've got suns, suns and polka dots.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: So nice.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Ding-dong, Seeker. Oh, who is it? Uncle Crisco. Oh, hi Uncle Crisco, says Seeker.
Now, the other night, we took a potion that we found in - in my - potion closet, or was it yours? The potion to go to a very magical place called Izzy's house.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Well, we don't - I don't have any more of those, but I bought a new one today that would take us to Izzy's house again.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Let's go, says Uncle Crisco.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, well - well Uncle Crisco...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yes?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, Seeker... said Uncle Crisco, do you have any potion that you would like to show me at your house?
Well, what about a potion to go to Izzy's attic?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: No. But that potion that would take us to Izzy's house, Izzy could tell you about her attic.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, she could?
Yes, she can, because she has a mouth.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: That's true. And what would she tell us about her attic?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. Well, where would you like to go? Said Seeker.
To Izzy's house.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Okay. Let's drink the Izzy's house potion. Sip, sip, sip.
Hulooska, Kulooska, Kaloom. Mooska, Gadooska, Kaloom.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Hulooska, Kulooska, Kaloom. Mooska, Gadooska, Kaloom. And then there was a 'whoosh!'
And floof! Suddenly, Seeker and Uncle Crisco were once again standing on a soft blue rug with white stars in the middle of Izzy's room.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Hi! Hi! My name is Izzy.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, hi Izzy, said Seeker.
Hi. Um, my attic has Legos, a drawing pad, a pen, paper, glue stick, suitcases, a chair, a work desk, lots of interesting things. A shelf, a floor, nails, a window. Those are all the things it has.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: And Uncle Crisco said, 'That sounds like a very interesting place, I would like to visit there sometime. Could you take us there?'
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: No, no. Only with a grown-up we can take you there with my dad tomorrow morning, if you would like.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh! Why don't we wait until then? And could we sleep here in your room while you're sleeping?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Yes. As long as you would be quiet and listen to whatever car you have in my Yodo.
Ah. Well, I don't know what a Yodo is, said Uncle Crisco.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: A Yodo has buttons. Lots of buttons. Like a pause one, one to turn it up and down, and one to push songs, and then there's - and there's also - a plug to charge it.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: I see, said Seeker. Well, I'm feeling... rather sleepy. Could you find me a place to sleep?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Yes, I can. Where - where could you sleep? Seeker, you could sleep with me. I'll make a nice nest.
Oh, thank you.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: You can sleep - you can sleep on Ginger's cage, if that would be comfy and you could rest your - rest your head on this cheese mouse.
Oh, a cheese mouse pillow? I've never had a pillow like that, said Seeker. Thank you. And Uncle Crisco said, well, that looks very cozy for Seeker, is there another bed for me somewhere?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: There is another bed. There's a slide bed in the dollhouse.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: A slide bed? I've never slept in a slide bed.
This is called a slide bed.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh, said Uncle Crisco. Do I slide down it?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: No, you sleep on the top with your feet lying, so you have to - so you have to - or you could sleep on the bush bed with an apple pillow.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A bush bed with an apple pillow? That sounds even nicer. But how am I going to get up on top of that shelf? Can you lift me up and put me there, please?
Um, or you could sleep in this crib.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: That crib looks very cozy, but I think I'm a little too big for it. I'm just a little mole, but I'm bigger than that crib. I'll try the bush bed.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Well, actually, this is Seeker's crib.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, but I thought Seeker was going to sleep - sleep in Ginger's cage.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, Seeker can sleep in here. If Seeker wants to sleep in here.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I think I fit just right in there. Thank you.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Then sleep - then sleep in there.
And so, Seeker crawled into the crib, and Uncle Crisco climbed up onto the bush bed with the strawberry pillow, and they both soon enough were sound asleep.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: What?
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: She woke up!
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: And her dad and me and Seeker and Uncle Crisco went to the attic.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: And they all went up to the attic. And what did they do up there?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: They played with Legos.
Oh, and what did they make?
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Robots.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: They made robots! What kind of robot did Seeker make?
Necklace robot. And then Uncle Crisco made a slide robot, and you made a baby robot, and you made - and I made a... sun kind of robot.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that sounds like a lot of fun. And did they do any other playing in the attic?
I don't know.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Izzy
transcript.text: But they did do some drawing in the attic.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what did they draw pictures of?
I don't know.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Well, Seeker likes to draw pictures of his friends, like the Crystal Mountain Caretaker and Gagok the magic paper bird. And he also has a magical flying stone skyship. And so he drew a picture of that for everyone to see.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then he said, 'You know, Uncle Crisco, I think we should be getting back. This has been a long adventure here at Izzy's house.'
And Seeker... um, what does your skyship do?
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Well, maybe, new friend Izzy the kiddo, you could come with us sometime and I could show you. But not today.
Oh yeah. Yeah, maybe tomorrow morning.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: That's a good idea. We'll do a skyship adventure then.
Yeah. I'm sick, so I don't want to show you cough, so I'll stay home for the rest of this day.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Okay, that's a good idea, said Seeker.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Because I have very bad hiccups and coughs.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I see. Oh, you'd better rest.
And stuffy nose.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Mmhmm. And so, Seeker and Uncle Crisco took the go-home potion, and then... there was a little pop sound and they vanished.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You can come back whenever you want.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, thank you.
Because Seeker has tons and tons of other going-here potions.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: He does have a lot of potions. All right.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Diddy-doo-dee. Diddy-doo-dee. Diddy-doo-dee.

characters.name: Seeker
characters.description: A brave dwarven explorer and mediator, he resides in a glowing castle hub—a multiversal dock for skyships anchored within the skyline of New York City. From this urban sanctum, he conducts silver slime experiments and tends to wandering cats amidst a massive library. Frequently swapping between a red coat and a mystical purple cloak, his braided red beard remains his most eccentric tool, capable of catching small fish. A master of fusion, he combines ancient stone-craft with high-velocity mechanics, utilizing a legendary magic ladder to scale the shifting architecture of the realms. Whether brewing portal potions, harvesting floof-berries in Limbo with gemstone goggles, or aiding Buddy the Mouse, this walnut-loving student of the stars remains the hero who orchestrated the daring rescue of the world.

characters.name: Uncle Grisco
characters.description: A nomadic mole scientist and science teacher, this subterranean inventor resides in a home brimming with wondrous experiments, serving as a devoted confidant to the gopher folk. Within his laboratory of glowing elixirs and prismatic shadows, he fuses volcanic lore with the study of light to translate the planet's restless movements and the scarred history of cosmic craters. A wandering sage who maps both deep tectonic veins and celestial paths, he frequently emerges from his sprawling tunnels to share ancient botanical secrets and astronomical insights with his friend Seeker.

characters.name: Isabella
characters.description: A towering Dimatar scholar and alchemist of Atsukeeseet, Mama Izzy is a clever kiddo at heart and a steadfast guardian of rooftop gardens and intricate dollhouses. Gifted with the power of flight, she navigates the open heights with aquatic grace, her massive frame often shielded by a special protective metal that glimmers even as she descends into the shadows. As a resourceful quartermaster, she employs red flashlights, gold-laced slimes, and pipe cleaners to mend a Lanternshark’s failing light, yet her generosity remains her hallmark; she freely shares her vast library, brokers homes for dragons, and teaches others to find the resonance in their own voices. Her most profound magic resides in her vast, soft blanket—a sanctuary of ultimate coziness—where she remains a brave, quick-witted protector to all who honor her singular request for absolute silence.

characters.name: Ginger
characters.description: A soft, round, and silver-coated feline, this exceptionally fluffy educator is one of Minnie and Mickey’s own kittens grown into a figure of quiet authority. Known for daintily distributing napkins with velvet paws and commuting to school behind the wheel, the gray-furred teacher balances his role on Nana’s welcoming committee with a life of polite, helpful service. Whether presiding over his humble sanctuary—the very cage Seeker once coveted—or sustaining himself on crunchy kibble and savory wet food, he remains a steady presence, most recently seen trading his classroom duties for the shadows of a secret closet hideout alongside Garfield.

characters.name: Crystal Mountain Caretaker
characters.description: A mysterious companion and frequent subject of Seeker’s sketches, this enigmatic friend often arrives in the quiet company of Hattie the Mouse.

characters.name: Gagok
characters.description: A magical paper bird and loyal companion to Seeker, this enchanted creature requires the essence of a specialized crystal to take wing and navigate the skies.

places.name: Izzy's House
places.description: Once the silent harbor of Jinjar’s return and the site of Rizzo and Izzy’s fateful council, this fortified sanctuary now serves as the primary destination reachable via a magical potion. Anchoring the company against the encroaching storm, the hallowed dwelling resembles a quaint hotel of labyrinthine rooms, featuring a celestial starry rug that blankets the ground where heroes once prepared for their departure to the dragon’s castle.

places.name: Izzy's Attic
places.description: Accessible only to those who swallow a shimmering potion and recite the ancient rhyme, this hidden workshop is a sanctuary of infinite creation. Within, sprawling Lego landscapes and intricate kits merge with a vibrant chaos of art supplies and heavy wooden work desks. Weathered suitcases, brimming with mystery, rest among the colorful bricks, marking a space where every tool and trinket is a portal to a new imagination.

places.name: Seeker's House
places.description: Perched at the edge of a mountain, this warm stone sanctuary—Seeker’s original residence and whimsical home shared with Uncle Crisco—has evolved from a secret vault into the purring, chaotic heart of a massive feline gala. Within its well-lit halls where Muppets gather for snacks and scientific discussions, lies a special hidden closet where Seeker brews an enchanted collection of potions that shimmer in every imaginable color.

places.name: Ginger's Cage
places.description: A spot in Izzy's room where Seeker considered resting on a cheese mouse pillow.

places.name: The Bush Bed
places.description: A comfortable sleeping spot on a shelf in Izzy's room featuring a fruit-themed pillow.
